steps.message:
# Dependency Inference Task

## Objective

Edit the build file(s) to include all necessary dependency configurations so the project builds and tests pass.

## Task Information

- **Language**: javascript
- **Project**: eslint/markdown
- **Build Files to Edit**: `package.json`

## Environment

```json
{
  "SDK": "node 23.x",
  "OS": "ubuntu-22.04"
}
```

## Instructions

1. Analyze the project structure in the repository to understand what external libraries are used
2. Review source code imports and usage to identify all required dependencies
3. Edit the build file(s) listed above to add correct dependency declarations
4. Include ALL dependencies needed for the project to build and run its tests

### Constraints

- Only edit the files listed in "Build Files to Edit"
- Limit edits to dependency-related configurations
- Do not modify source code files
I'll help you identify and add the necessary dependencies to make the project build and test properly. Let me start by exploring the project structure.

file.content:
{
  "name": "@eslint/markdown",
  "version": "6.2.1",
  "description": "The official ESLint language plugin for Markdown",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": {
    "name": "Brandon Mills",
    "url": "https://github.com/btmills"
  },
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/esm/index.js",
  "types": "dist/esm/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/esm/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/esm/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "repository": "eslint/markdown",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "eslint",
    "eslintplugin",
    "markdown",
    "lint",
    "linter"
  ],
  "gitHooks": {
    "pre-commit": "lint-staged"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.js": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "!(*.{js,md})": "prettier --write --ignore-unknown",
    "{src/rules/*.js,tools/update-rules-docs.js,README.md}": "npm run build:update-rules-docs"
  },

{
  "name": "@eslint/markdown",
  "version": "6.2.1",
  "description": "The official ESLint language plugin for Markdown",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": {
    "name": "Brandon Mills",
    "url": "https://github.com/btmills"
  },
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/esm/index.js",
  "types": "dist/esm/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/esm/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/esm/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "repository": "eslint/markdown",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "eslint",
    "eslintplugin",
    "markdown",
    "lint",
    "linter"
  ],
  "gitHooks": {
    "pre-commit": "lint-staged"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.js": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "!(*.{js,md})": "prettier --write --ignore-unknown",
    "{src/rules/*.js,tools/update-rules-docs.js,README.md}": "npm run build:update-rules-docs"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "eslint . && eslint -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "lint:fix": "eslint --fix . && eslint --fix -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "fmt": "prettier --write .",
    "fmt:check": "prettier --check .",
    "build:dedupe-types": "node tools/dedupe-types.js dist/esm/index.js",
    "build:rules": "node tools/build-rules.js",
    "build:update-rules-docs": "node tools/update-rules-docs.js",
    "build": "npm run build:rules && rollup -c && npm run build:dedupe-types && tsc -p tsconfig.esm.json && npm run build:update-rules-docs",
    "build:readme": "node tools/update-readme.js",
    "prepare": "node ./npm-prepare.cjs && npm run build",
    "test": "c8 mocha \"tests/**/*.test.js\" --timeout 30000",
    "test:jsr": "npx jsr@latest publish --dry-run",
    "test:types": "tsc -p tests/types/tsconfig.json"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/core": "^0.6.0",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.4.0",
    "@types/eslint": "^9.6.0",
    "c8": "^10.1.2",
    "chai": "^5.1.1",
    "dedent": "^1.5.3",
    "eslint": "^9.15.0",
    "eslint-config-eslint": "^11.0.0",
    "eslint-plugin-eslint-plugin": "^6.3.2",
    "globals": "^15.1.0",
    "got": "^14.4.2",
    "lint-staged": "^15.2.9",
    "mocha": "^10.6.0",
    "prettier": "^3.3.3",
    "rollup": "^4.19.0",
    "rollup-plugin-copy": "^3.5.0",
    "typescript": "^5.5.4",
    "yorkie": "^2.0.0"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^18.18.0 || ^20.9.0 || >=21.1.0"
  }
}

1→/**
     2→ * @fileoverview Enables the processor for Markdown file extensions.
     3→ * @author Brandon Mills
     4→ */
     5→
     6→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Imports
     8→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→import { processor } from "./processor.js";
    11→import { MarkdownLanguage } from "./language/markdown-language.js";
    12→import recommendedRules from "./build/recommended-config.js";
    13→import rules from "./build/rules.js";
    14→
    15→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→// Type Definitions
    17→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18→
    19→/** @typedef {import("eslint").Linter.RulesRecord} RulesRecord*/
    20→/** @typedef {import("eslint").Linter.Config} Config*/
    21→/** @typedef {import("eslint").ESLint.Plugin} Plugin */
    22→/** @typedef {import("eslint").Rule.RuleModule} RuleModule */
    23→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").Language} Language */
    24→
    25→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→// Exports
    27→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→
    29→/** @type {RulesRecord} */
    30→const processorRulesConfig = {
    31→	// The Markdown parser automatically trims trailing
    32→	// newlines from code blocks.
    33→	"eol-last": "off",
    34→
    35→	// In code snippets and examples, these rules are often
    36→	// counterproductive to clarity and brevity.
    37→	"no-undef": "off",
    38→	"no-unused-expressions": "off",
    39→	"no-unused-vars": "off",
    40→	"padded-blocks": "off",
    41→
    42→	// Adding a "use strict" directive at the top of every
    43→	// code block is tedious and distracting. The config
    44→	// opts into strict mode parsing without the directive.
    45→	strict: "off",
    46→
    47→	// The processor will not receive a Unicode Byte Order
    48→	// Mark from the Markdown parser.
    49→	"unicode-bom": "off",
    50→};
    51→
    52→let recommendedPlugins, processorPlugins;
    53→
    54→const plugin = {
    55→	meta: {
    56→		name: "@eslint/markdown",
    57→		version: "6.2.1", // x-release-please-version
    58→	},
    59→	processors: {
    60→		markdown: processor,
    61→	},
    62→	languages: {
    63→		commonmark: new MarkdownLanguage({ mode: "commonmark" }),
    64→		gfm: new MarkdownLanguage({ mode: "gfm" }),
    65→	},
    66→	rules,
    67→	configs: {
    68→		"recommended-legacy": {
    69→			plugins: ["markdown"],
    70→			overrides: [
    71→				{
    72→					files: ["*.md"],
    73→					processor: "markdown/markdown",
    74→				},
    75→				{
    76→					files: ["**/*.md/**"],
    77→					parserOptions: {
    78→						ecmaFeatures: {
    79→							// Adding a "use strict" directive at the top of
    80→							// every code block is tedious and distracting, so
    81→							// opt into strict mode parsing without the
    82→							// directive.
    83→							impliedStrict: true,
    84→						},
    85→					},
    86→					rules: {
    87→						...processorRulesConfig,
    88→					},
    89→				},
    90→			],
    91→		},
    92→		recommended: [
    93→			{
    94→				name: "markdown/recommended",
    95→				files: ["**/*.md"],
    96→				language: "markdown/commonmark",
    97→				plugins: (recommendedPlugins = {}),
    98→				rules: recommendedRules,
    99→			},
   100→		],
   101→		processor: [
   102→			{
   103→				name: "markdown/recommended/plugin",
   104→				plugins: (processorPlugins = {}),
   105→			},
   106→			{
   107→				name: "markdown/recommended/processor",
   108→				files: ["**/*.md"],
   109→				processor: "markdown/markdown",
   110→			},
   111→			{
   112→				name: "markdown/recommended/code-blocks",
   113→				files: ["**/*.md/**"],
   114→				languageOptions: {
   115→					parserOptions: {
   116→						ecmaFeatures: {
   117→							// Adding a "use strict" directive at the top of
   118→							// every code block is tedious and distracting, so
   119→							// opt into strict mode parsing without the
   120→							// directive.
   121→							impliedStrict: true,
   122→						},
   123→					},
   124→				},
   125→				rules: {
   126→					...processorRulesConfig,
   127→				},
   128→			},
   129→		],
   130→	},
   131→};
   132→
   133→// @ts-expect-error
   134→recommendedPlugins.markdown = processorPlugins.markdown = plugin;
   135→
   136→export default plugin;
   137→

1→/**
     2→ * @fileoverview Enables the processor for Markdown file extensions.
     3→ * @author Brandon Mills
     4→ */
     5→
     6→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Imports
     8→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→import { processor } from "./processor.js";
    11→import { MarkdownLanguage } from "./language/markdown-language.js";
    12→import recommendedRules from "./build/recommended-config.js";
    13→import rules from "./build/rules.js";
    14→
    15→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→// Type Definitions
    17→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18→
    19→/** @typedef {import("eslint").Linter.RulesRecord} RulesRecord*/
    20→/** @typedef {import("eslint").Linter.Config} Config*/
    21→/** @typedef {import("eslint").ESLint.Plugin} Plugin */
    22→/** @typedef {import("eslint").Rule.RuleModule} RuleModule */
    23→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").Language} Language */
    24→
    25→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→// Exports
    27→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→
    29→/** @type {RulesRecord} */
    30→const processorRulesConfig = {
    31→	// The Markdown parser automatically trims trailing
    32→	// newlines from code blocks.
    33→	"eol-last": "off",
    34→
    35→	// In code snippets and examples, these rules are often
    36→	// counterproductive to clarity and brevity.
    37→	"no-undef": "off",
    38→	"no-unused-expressions": "off",
    39→	"no-unused-vars": "off",
    40→	"padded-blocks": "off",
    41→
    42→	// Adding a "use strict" directive at the top of every
    43→	// code block is tedious and distracting. The config
    44→	// opts into strict mode parsing without the directive.
    45→	strict: "off",
    46→
    47→	// The processor will not receive a Unicode Byte Order
    48→	// Mark from the Markdown parser.
    49→	"unicode-bom": "off",
    50→};
    51→
    52→let recommendedPlugins, processorPlugins;
    53→
    54→const plugin = {
    55→	meta: {
    56→		name: "@eslint/markdown",
    57→		version: "6.2.1", // x-release-please-version
    58→	},
    59→	processors: {
    60→		markdown: processor,
    61→	},
    62→	languages: {
    63→		commonmark: new MarkdownLanguage({ mode: "commonmark" }),
    64→		gfm: new MarkdownLanguage({ mode: "gfm" }),
    65→	},
    66→	rules,
    67→	configs: {
    68→		"recommended-legacy": {
    69→			plugins: ["markdown"],
    70→			overrides: [
    71→				{
    72→					files: ["*.md"],
    73→					processor: "markdown/markdown",
    74→				},
    75→				{
    76→					files: ["**/*.md/**"],
    77→					parserOptions: {
    78→						ecmaFeatures: {
    79→							// Adding a "use strict" directive at the top of
    80→							// every code block is tedious and distracting, so
    81→							// opt into strict mode parsing without the
    82→							// directive.
    83→							impliedStrict: true,
    84→						},
    85→					},
    86→					rules: {
    87→						...processorRulesConfig,
    88→					},
    89→				},
    90→			],
    91→		},
    92→		recommended: [
    93→			{
    94→				name: "markdown/recommended",
    95→				files: ["**/*.md"],
    96→				language: "markdown/commonmark",
    97→				plugins: (recommendedPlugins = {}),
    98→				rules: recommendedRules,
    99→			},
   100→		],
   101→		processor: [
   102→			{
   103→				name: "markdown/recommended/plugin",
   104→				plugins: (processorPlugins = {}),
   105→			},
   106→			{
   107→				name: "markdown/recommended/processor",
   108→				files: ["**/*.md"],
   109→				processor: "markdown/markdown",
   110→			},
   111→			{
   112→				name: "markdown/recommended/code-blocks",
   113→				files: ["**/*.md/**"],
   114→				languageOptions: {
   115→					parserOptions: {
   116→						ecmaFeatures: {
   117→							// Adding a "use strict" directive at the top of
   118→							// every code block is tedious and distracting, so
   119→							// opt into strict mode parsing without the
   120→							// directive.
   121→							impliedStrict: true,
   122→						},
   123→					},
   124→				},
   125→				rules: {
   126→					...processorRulesConfig,
   127→				},
   128→			},
   129→		],
   130→	},
   131→};
   132→
   133→// @ts-expect-error
   134→recommendedPlugins.markdown = processorPlugins.markdown = plugin;
   135→
   136→export default plugin;
   137→

1→/**
     2→ * @fileoverview Functions to fix up rules to provide missing methods on the `context` object.
     3→ * @author Nicholas C. Zakas
     4→ */
     5→
     6→/* eslint class-methods-use-this: 0 -- Required to complete interface. */
     7→
     8→//------------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→// Imports
    10→//------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11→
    12→import { MarkdownSourceCode } from "./markdown-source-code.js";
    13→import { fromMarkdown } from "mdast-util-from-markdown";
    14→import { gfmFromMarkdown } from "mdast-util-gfm";
    15→import { gfm } from "micromark-extension-gfm";
    16→
    17→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18→// Types
    19→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20→
    21→/** @typedef {import("mdast").Root} RootNode */
    22→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").Language} Language */
    23→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").File} File */
    24→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").ParseResult<RootNode>} ParseResult */
    25→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").OkParseResult<RootNode>} OkParseResult */
    26→/** @typedef {import("@eslint/core").SyntaxElement} SyntaxElement */
    27→/** @typedef {"commonmark"|"gfm"} ParserMode */
    28→
    29→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→// Exports
    31→//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→
    33→/**
    34→ * Markdown Language Object
    35→ * @implements {Language}
    36→ */
    37→export class MarkdownLanguage {
    38→	/**
    39→	 * The type of file to read.
    40→	 * @type {"text"}
    41→	 */
    42→	fileType = "text";
    43→
    44→	/**
    45→	 * The line number at which the parser starts counting.
    46→	 * @type {0|1}
    47→	 */
    48→	lineStart = 1;
    49→
    50→	/**
    51→	 * The column number at which the parser starts counting.
    52→	 * @type {0|1}
    53→	 */
    54→	columnStart = 1;
    55→
    56→	/**
    57→	 * The name of the key that holds the type of the node.
    58→	 * @type {string}
    59→	 */
    60→	nodeTypeKey = "type";
    61→
    62→	/**
    63→	 * The Markdown parser mode.
    64→	 * @type {ParserMode}
    65→	 */
    66→	#mode = "commonmark";
    67→
    68→	/**
    69→	 * Creates a new instance.
    70→	 * @param {Object} options The options to use for this instance.
    71→	 * @param {ParserMode} [options.mode] The Markdown parser mode to use.
    72→	 */
    73→	constructor({ mode } = {}) {
    74→		if (mode) {
    75→			this.#mode = mode;
    76→		}
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	/* eslint-disable no-unused-vars -- Required to complete interface. */
    80→	/**
    81→	 * Validates the language options.
    82→	 * @param {Object} languageOptions The language options to validate.
    83→	 * @returns {void}
    84→	 * @throws {Error} When the language options are invalid.
    85→	 */
    86→	validateLanguageOptions(languageOptions) {
    87→		// no-op
    88→	}
    89→	/* eslint-enable no-unused-vars -- Required to complete interface. */
    90→
    91→	/**
    92→	 * Parses the given file into an AST.
    93→	 * @param {File} file The virtual file to parse.
    94→	 * @returns {ParseResult} The result of parsing.
    95→	 */
    96→	parse(file) {
    97→		// Note: BOM already removed
    98→		const text = /** @type {string} */ (file.body);
    99→
   100→		/*
   101→		 * Check for parsing errors first. If there's a parsing error, nothing
   102→		 * else can happen. However, a parsing error does not throw an error
   103→		 * from this method - it's just considered a fatal error message, a
   104→		 * problem that ESLint identified just like any other.
   105→		 */
   106→		try {
   107→			const options =
   108→				this.#mode === "gfm"
   109→					? {
   110→							extensions: [gfm()],
   111→							mdastExtensions: [gfmFromMarkdown()],
   112→						}
   113→					: { extensions: [] };
   114→			const root = fromMarkdown(text, options);
   115→
   116→			return {
   117→				ok: true,
   118→				ast: root,
   119→			};
   120→		} catch (ex) {
   121→			return {
   122→				ok: false,
   123→				errors: [ex],
   124→			};
   125→		}
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	/**
   129→	 * Creates a new `MarkdownSourceCode` object from the given information.
   130→	 * @param {File} file The virtual file to create a `MarkdownSourceCode` object from.
   131→	 * @param {OkParseResult} parseResult The result returned from `parse()`.
   132→	 * @returns {MarkdownSourceCode} The new `MarkdownSourceCode` object.
   133→	 */
   134→	createSourceCode(file, parseResult) {
   135→		return new MarkdownSourceCode({
   136→			text: /** @type {string} */ (file.body),
   137→			ast: parseResult.ast,
   138→		});
   139→	}
   140→}
   141→

steps.message:
Perfect! I can see the test is failing because `mdast-util-from-markdown` is not installed. Let me analyze all the missing dependencies based on my review of the source code:

**From source code (src/):**
- `mdast-util-from-markdown` - used in processor.js and markdown-language.js
- `mdast-util-gfm` - used in markdown-language.js
- `micromark-extension-gfm` - used in markdown-language.js
- `@eslint/plugin-kit` - used in markdown-source-code.js

**From tests:**
- `semver` - used in tests/examples/all.test.js

Let me check what versions should be used by looking at similar ESLint packages and the npm registry:

{
  "name": "@eslint/markdown",
  "version": "6.2.1",
  "description": "The official ESLint language plugin for Markdown",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": {
    "name": "Brandon Mills",
    "url": "https://github.com/btmills"
  },
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/esm/index.js",
  "types": "dist/esm/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/esm/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/esm/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "repository": "eslint/markdown",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "eslint",
    "eslintplugin",
    "markdown",
    "lint",
    "linter"
  ],
  "gitHooks": {
    "pre-commit": "lint-staged"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.js": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "!(*.{js,md})": "prettier --write --ignore-unknown",
    "{src/rules/*.js,tools/update-rules-docs.js,README.md}": "npm run build:update-rules-docs"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "eslint . && eslint -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "lint:fix": "eslint --fix . && eslint --fix -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "fmt": "prettier --write .",
    "fmt:check": "prettier --check .",
    "build:dedupe-types": "node tools/dedupe-types.js dist/esm/index.js",
    "build:rules": "node tools/build-rules.js",
    "build:update-rules-docs": "node tools/update-rules-docs.js",
    "build": "npm run build:rules && rollup -c && npm run build:dedupe-types && tsc -p tsconfig.esm.json && npm run build:update-rules-docs",
    "build:readme": "node tools/update-readme.js",
    "prepare": "node ./npm-prepare.cjs && npm run build",
    "test": "c8 mocha \"tests/**/*.test.js\" --timeout 30000",
    "test:jsr": "npx jsr@latest publish --dry-run",
    "test:types": "tsc -p tests/types/tsconfig.json"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/core": "^0.6.0",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.4.0",
    "@types/eslint": "^9.6.0",
    "c8": "^10.1.2",
    "chai": "^5.1.1",
    "dedent": "^1.5.3",
    "eslint": "^9.15.0",
    "eslint-config-eslint": "^11.0.0",
    "eslint-plugin-eslint-plugin": "^6.3.2",
    "globals": "^15.1.0",
    "got": "^14.4.2",
    "lint-staged": "^15.2.9",
    "mocha": "^10.6.0",
    "prettier": "^3.3.3",
    "rollup": "^4.19.0",
    "rollup-plugin-copy": "^3.5.0",
    "typescript": "^5.5.4",
    "yorkie": "^2.0.0"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^18.18.0 || ^20.9.0 || >=21.1.0"
  }
}

{
  "name": "@eslint/markdown",
  "version": "6.2.1",
  "description": "The official ESLint language plugin for Markdown",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": {
    "name": "Brandon Mills",
    "url": "https://github.com/btmills"
  },
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/esm/index.js",
  "types": "dist/esm/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/esm/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/esm/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "repository": "eslint/markdown",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "eslint",
    "eslintplugin",
    "markdown",
    "lint",
    "linter"
  ],
  "gitHooks": {
    "pre-commit": "lint-staged"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.js": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "!(*.{js,md})": "prettier --write --ignore-unknown",
    "{src/rules/*.js,tools/update-rules-docs.js,README.md}": "npm run build:update-rules-docs"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "eslint . && eslint -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "lint:fix": "eslint --fix . && eslint --fix -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "fmt": "prettier --write .",
    "fmt:check": "prettier --check .",
    "build:dedupe-types": "node tools/dedupe-types.js dist/esm/index.js",
    "build:rules": "node tools/build-rules.js",
    "build:update-rules-docs": "node tools/update-rules-docs.js",
    "build": "npm run build:rules && rollup -c && npm run build:dedupe-types && tsc -p tsconfig.esm.json && npm run build:update-rules-docs",
    "build:readme": "node tools/update-readme.js",
    "prepare": "node ./npm-prepare.cjs && npm run build",
    "test": "c8 mocha \"tests/**/*.test.js\" --timeout 30000",
    "test:jsr": "npx jsr@latest publish --dry-run",
    "test:types": "tsc -p tests/types/tsconfig.json"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/core": "^0.6.0",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.4.0",
    "@types/eslint": "^9.6.0",
    "c8": "^10.1.2",
    "chai": "^5.1.1",
    "dedent": "^1.5.3",
    "eslint": "^9.15.0",
    "eslint-config-eslint": "^11.0.0",
    "eslint-plugin-eslint-plugin": "^6.3.2",
    "globals": "^15.1.0",
    "got": "^14.4.2",
    "lint-staged": "^15.2.9",
    "mocha": "^10.6.0",
    "prettier": "^3.3.3",
    "rollup": "^4.19.0",
    "rollup-plugin-copy": "^3.5.0",
    "typescript": "^5.5.4",
    "yorkie": "^2.0.0"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^18.18.0 || ^20.9.0 || >=21.1.0"
  }
}

{
  "name": "@eslint/markdown",
  "version": "6.2.1",
  "description": "The official ESLint language plugin for Markdown",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": {
    "name": "Brandon Mills",
    "url": "https://github.com/btmills"
  },
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/esm/index.js",
  "types": "dist/esm/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/esm/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/esm/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@eslint/plugin-kit": "^0.6.0",
    "mdast-util-from-markdown": "^2.0.0",
    "mdast-util-gfm": "^3.0.0",
    "micromark-extension-gfm": "^3.0.0"
  },
  "repository": "eslint/markdown",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/eslint/markdown#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "eslint",
    "eslintplugin",
    "markdown",
    "lint",
    "linter"
  ],
  "gitHooks": {
    "pre-commit": "lint-staged"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.js": [
      "eslint --fix",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "!(*.{js,md})": "prettier --write --ignore-unknown",
    "{src/rules/*.js,tools/update-rules-docs.js,README.md}": "npm run build:update-rules-docs"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "eslint . && eslint -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "lint:fix": "eslint --fix . && eslint --fix -c eslint.config-content.js .",
    "fmt": "prettier --write .",
    "fmt:check": "prettier --check .",
    "build:dedupe-types": "node tools/dedupe-types.js dist/esm/index.js",
    "build:rules": "node tools/build-rules.js",
    "build:update-rules-docs": "node tools/update-rules-docs.js",
    "build": "npm run build:rules && rollup -c && npm run build:dedupe-types && tsc -p tsconfig.esm.json && npm run build:update-rules-docs",
    "build:readme": "node tools/update-readme.js",
    "prepare": "node ./npm-prepare.cjs && npm run build",
    "test": "c8 mocha \"tests/**/*.test.js\" --timeout 30000",
    "test:jsr": "npx jsr@latest publish --dry-run",
    "test:types": "tsc -p tests/types/tsconfig.json"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/core": "^0.6.0",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.4.0",
    "@types/eslint": "^9.6.0",
    "c8": "^10.1.2",
    "chai": "^5.1.1",
    "dedent": "^1.5.3",
    "eslint": "^9.15.0",
    "eslint-config-eslint": "^11.0.0",
    "eslint-plugin-eslint-plugin": "^6.3.2",
    "globals": "^15.1.0",
    "got": "^14.4.2",
    "lint-staged": "^15.2.9",
    "mocha": "^10.6.0",
    "prettier": "^3.3.3",
    "rollup": "^4.19.0",
    "rollup-plugin-copy": "^3.5.0",
    "semver": "^7.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.5.4",
    "yorkie": "^2.0.0"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^18.18.0 || ^20.9.0 || >=21.1.0"
  }
}

steps.message:
Perfect! Let me create a comprehensive summary of the changes made:

## Summary

I have successfully identified and added all necessary dependencies to the `package.json` file for the eslint/markdown project. Here's what was added:

### Dependencies Added (Runtime)
1. **`@eslint/plugin-kit`: ^0.6.0** - Used in `src/language/markdown-source-code.js` for plugin utilities
2. **`mdast-util-from-markdown`: ^2.0.0** - Used in `src/processor.js` and `src/language/markdown-language.js` for parsing Markdown to AST
3. **`mdast-util-gfm`: ^3.0.0** - Used in `src/language/markdown-language.js` for GitHub Flavored Markdown support
4. **`micromark-extension-gfm`: ^3.0.0** - Used in `src/language/markdown-language.js` for GFM tokenization

### Dependencies Added (DevDependencies)
5. **`semver`: ^7.0.0** - Used in `tests/examples/all.test.js` for semantic version comparisons

### Verification
✅ All packages are installed in `node_modules`  
✅ `package.json` has been updated with a new `dependencies` section  
✅ `semver` has been added to `devDependencies`  
✅ All dependency versions use appropriate caret (`^`) ranges for flexibility

The dependencies were identified by analyzing:
- Source code imports in `src/` directory
- Test file imports in `tests/` directory
- Import statements from external packages (excluding relative imports and Node.js built-in modules)
